definition: antecedent to οἷος, as ἀοιδοῦ τοιοῦδ᾽ οἷος ὅδ᾽ ἐστί of such a  minstrel as is this one, (Odyssey by Homer); absolute, ἀλλ᾽ ὅδ᾽ ἐγὼ τοιόσδε here am I such as you see , (Odyssey by Homer):—;also, so great, so noble, so bad , τοιάδε λαίφη such  clothes, i. e. so bad , (Odyssey by Homer); τοσόσδε καὶ τοιόσδε (Herdotus Historicus):—;also with a qualifying word, τοιόσδ᾽ ἠμὲν δέμας ἠδὲ καὶ ἔργα such  both in form and works, (Odyssey by Homer):—;with the Art., ὁ τ. ἀνήρ (Aeschulus Tragicus), etc.; οἱ τοιοίδε (Sophocles Tragicus); ἐν τῷ τοιῷδε in such  circumstances, (Herdotus Historicus):—;the sense is made more indefinite in τοιόσδε τις such a  one, (Thucydides):—;neuter plural τοιάδε as follows , τοιαῦτα as aforesaid , (Herdotus Historicus) (ML)
